title: Deploy a log-collector DaemonSet on every node
scenario: Your platform team needs a `log-collector` DaemonSet that runs a logging agent on every node in the cluster. The simulator has 2 nodes. Deploy the DaemonSet and verify it schedules one pod per node.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: log-collector
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: log-collector
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: log-collector
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: log-collector
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: log-agent
        image: alpine:3.18
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "while true; do echo collecting logs; sleep 5; done"]
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "100m"
            memory: "64Mi"

hints.text: The starter YAML is already correct — just apply it. A DaemonSet automatically places one pod on every schedulable node.
After applying, run `kubectl get daemonset log-collector` and confirm `DESIRED` and `READY` both show 2.
If a pod is missing from a node, check for taints on that node with `kubectl describe node <node-name>`. You may need a toleration to schedule on tainted nodes.

solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: log-collector
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: log-collector
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: log-collector
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: log-collector
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: log-agent
        image: alpine:3.18
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "while true; do echo collecting logs; sleep 5; done"]
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "100m"
            memory: "64Mi"
